L. 89–384, § 3(c)80 Stat. 105July 30, 1965Apr. 8, 1966“If—“(1)an individual was eligible to enroll under section 1837(c) of the Social Security Act [] before , but failed to enroll before such date, and42 U.S.C. 1395p(c)June 1, 1966“(2)it is shown to the satisfaction of the Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare [now Health and Human Services] that there was good cause for such failure to enroll before ,June 1, 1966such individual may enroll pursuant to this subsection at any time before . children.heading: Generally; regulations
children.label: a Generally; regulations
children.text: An individual may enroll in the insurance program established by this part only in such manner and form as may be prescribed by regulations, and only during an enrollment period prescribed in or under this section.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 96–499, title IX, § 945(a)94 Stat. 2642Dec. 5, 1980
children.label: b Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 96–499, title IX, § 945(a)94 Stat. 2642Dec. 5, 1980

children.heading: Initial general enrollment period; eligible individuals beforeMarch 1, 1966
children.label: c Initial general enrollment period; eligible individuals beforeMarch 1, 1966
children.text: In the case of individuals who first satisfy paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1395(a) of this title before , the initial general enrollment period shall begin on the first day of the second month which begins after , and shall end on . For purposes of this subsection and subsection (d), an individual who has attained age 65 and who satisfies paragraph (1) of section 1395(a) of this title but not paragraph (2) of such section shall be treated as satisfying such paragraph (1) on the first day on which he is (or on filing application would have been) entitled to hospital insurance benefits under part A.ooMarch 1, 1966July 30, 1965May 31, 1966

children.heading: Eligible individuals on or afterMarch 1, 1966
children.label: d Eligible individuals on or afterMarch 1, 1966
children.text: In the case of an individual who first satisfies paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1395(a) of this title on or after , his initial enrollment period shall begin on the first day of the third month before the month in which he first satisfies such paragraphs and shall end seven months later. Where the Secretary finds that an individual who has attained age 65 failed to enroll under this part during his initial enrollment period (based on a determination by the Secretary of the month in which such individual attained age 65), because such individual (relying on documentary evidence) was mistaken as to his correct date of birth, the Secretary shall establish for such individual an initial enrollment period based on his attaining age 65 at the time shown in such documentary evidence (with a coverage period determined under as though he had attained such age at that time).oMarch 1, 1966section 1395q of this title

children.heading: General enrollment period
children.label: e General enrollment period
children.text: There shall be a general enrollment period during the period beginning on January 1 and ending on March 31 of each year.

children.heading: Individuals deemed enrolled in medical insurance program
children.label: f Individuals deemed enrolled in medical insurance program

children.text: who is eligible under section 1395(a) of this title to enroll in the medical insurance program by reason of entitlement to hospital insurance benefits as described in paragraph (1) of such section, ando
whose initial enrollment period under subsection (d) begins after , andMarch 31, 1973
who is residing in the United States, exclusive of Puerto Rico,

in the case of an individual who satisfies subsection (f) by reason of entitlement to disability insurance benefits described in , his initial enrollment period shall begin on the first day of the later of (A) April 1973 or (B) the third month before the 25th month of such entitlement, and shall reoccur with each continuous period of eligibility (as defined in ) and upon attainment of age 65;section 426(b) of this titlesection 1395r(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an individual who is entitled to monthly benefits under section 402 or 423 of this title on the first day of his initial enrollment period or becomes entitled to monthly benefits under during the first 3 months of such period, his enrollment shall be deemed to have occurred in the third month of his initial enrollment period, andsection 402 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an individual who is not entitled to benefits under on the first day of his initial enrollment period and does not become so entitled during the first 3 months of such period, his enrollment shall be deemed to have occurred in the month in which he files the application establishing his entitlement to hospital insurance benefits provided such filing occurs during the last 4 months of his initial enrollment period; andsection 402 of this title

in the case of an individual who would otherwise satisfy subsection (f) but does not establish his entitlement to hospital insurance benefits until after the last day of his initial enrollment period (as defined in subsection (d) of this section), his enrollment shall be deemed to have occurred on the first day of the earlier of the then current or immediately succeeding general enrollment period (as defined in subsection (e) of this section).

children.heading: Waiver of enrollment period requirements where individual’s rights were prejudiced by administrative error or inaction
children.label: h Waiver of enrollment period requirements where individual’s rights were prejudiced by administrative error or inaction
children.text: In any case where the Secretary finds that an individual’s enrollment or nonenrollment in the insurance program established by this part or part A pursuant to is unintentional, inadvertent, or erroneous and is the result of the error, misrepresentation, or inaction of an officer, employee, or agent of the Federal Government, or its instrumentalities, the Secretary may take such action (including the designation for such individual of a special initial or subsequent enrollment period, with a coverage period determined on the basis thereof and with appropriate adjustments of premiums) as may be necessary to correct or eliminate the effects of such error, misrepresentation, or inaction.section 1395i–2 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: at the time the individual first satisfies paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1395(a) of this title, is enrolled in a group health plan described in by reason of the individual’s (or the individual’s spouse’s) current employment status, andosection 1395y(b)(1)(A)(v) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: has elected not to enroll (or to be deemed enrolled) under this section during the individual’s initial enrollment period,

children.label: i
children.text: has enrolled (or has been deemed to have enrolled) in the medical insurance program established under this part during the individual’s initial enrollment period, or (ii) is an individual described in paragraph (1)(A);

children.label: B
children.text: has enrolled in such program during any subsequent special enrollment period under this subsection during which the individual was not enrolled in a group health plan described in by reason of the individual’s (or individual’s spouse’s) current employment status; andsection 1395y(b)(1)(A)(v) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: has not terminated enrollment under this section at any time at which the individual is not enrolled in such a group health plan by reason of the individual’s (or individual’s spouse’s) current employment status,

children.label: A
children.text: The special enrollment period referred to in the first sentences of paragraphs (1) and (2) is the period including each month during any part of which the individual is enrolled in a group health plan described in by reason of current employment status ending with the last day of the eighth consecutive month in which the individual is at no time so enrolled.section 1395y(b)(1)(A)(v) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The special enrollment period referred to in the second sentences of paragraphs (1) and (2) is the period including each month during any part of which the individual is enrolled in a large group health plan (as that term is defined in ) by reason of the individual’s current employment status (or the current employment status of a family member of the individual) ending with the last day of the eighth consecutive month in which the individual is at no time so enrolled.section 1395y(b)(1)(B)(iii) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: is enrolled in a group health plan described in by reason of the individual’s current or former employment or by reason of the current or former employment status of a member of the individual’s family, andsection 1395y(b)(1)(A)(v) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: has elected not to enroll (or to be deemed enrolled) under this section during the individual’s initial enrollment period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: whose continuous enrollment under such group health plan is involuntarily terminated at a time when the enrollment under the plan is not by reason of the individual’s current employment or by reason of the current employment of a member of the individual’s family,

children.label: B
children.text: The special enrollment period referred to in subparagraph (A) is the 6-month period beginning on the first day of the month which includes the date of the enrollment termination described in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Special rules for individuals with ALS
children.label: j Special rules for individuals with ALS

children.text: The initial enrollment period under subsection (d) shall begin on the first day of the first month in which the individual satisfies the requirement of section 1395(a)(1) of this title.o
In applying subsection (g)(1), the initial enrollment period shall begin on the first day of the first month of entitlement to disability insurance benefits referred to in such subsection.

children.heading: Special enrollment period for certain volunteers serving outside United States
children.label: k Special enrollment period for certain volunteers serving outside United States
A
children.text: at the time the individual first satisfies paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1395(a) of this title, is described in paragraph (3), and has elected not to enroll (or to be deemed enrolled) under this section during the individual’s initial enrollment period; oro

children.label: B
children.text: has terminated enrollment under this section during a month in which the individual is described in paragraph (3),

The special enrollment period described in this paragraph is the 6-month period beginning on the first day of the month which includes the date that the individual is no longer described in paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: that covers at least a 12-month period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that is sponsored by an organization described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such Code; and

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrates health insurance coverage while serving in the program.

children.heading: Special enrollment period for disabled TRICARE beneficiaries
children.label: l Special enrollment period for disabled TRICARE beneficiaries

children.text: In the case of any individual who is a covered beneficiary (as defined in ) at the time the individual is entitled to part A under or and who is eligible to enroll but who has elected not to enroll (or to be deemed enrolled) during the individual’s initial enrollment period, there shall be a special enrollment period described in paragraph (2).section 1072(5) of title 10section 426(b) of this titlesection 426–1 of this title

The special enrollment period described in this paragraph, with respect to an individual, is the 12-month period beginning on the day after the last day of the initial enrollment period of the individual or, if later, the 12-month period beginning with the month the individual is notified of enrollment under this section.

In the case of an individual who enrolls during the special enrollment period provided under paragraph (1), the coverage period under this part shall begin on the first day of the month in which the individual enrolls, or, at the option of the individual, the first month after the end of the individual’s initial enrollment period.
An individual may only enroll during the special enrollment period provided under paragraph (1) one time during the individual’s lifetime.

The Secretary shall ensure that the materials relating to coverage under this part that are provided to an individual described in paragraph (1) prior to the individual’s initial enrollment period contain information concerning the impact of not enrolling under this part, including the impact on health care benefits under the TRICARE program under chapter 55 of title 10.

The Secretary of Defense shall collaborate with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Commissioner of Social Security to provide for the accurate identification of individuals described in paragraph (1). The Secretary of Defense shall provide such individuals with notification with respect to this subsection. The Secretary of Defense shall collaborate with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Commissioner of Social Security to ensure appropriate follow up pursuant to any notification provided under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Special enrollment periods for exceptional circumstances
children.label: m Special enrollment periods for exceptional circumstances
children.text: Beginning , the Secretary may establish special enrollment periods in the case of individuals who satisfy paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1395(a) of this title and meet such exceptional conditions as the Secretary may provide.January 1, 2023o

children.heading: Enrollment for individuals only eligible for coverage of immunosuppressive drugs
children.label: n Enrollment for individuals only eligible for coverage of immunosuppressive drugs

children.text: Any individual who is eligible for coverage of immunosuppressive drugs under section 1395(b) of this title may enroll or be deemed to have enrolled only in such manner and form as may be prescribed by regulations, and only during an enrollment period described in this subsection.o

An individual described in paragraph (1) whose entitlement for hospital insurance benefits under part A ends by reason of prior to , may enroll beginning on , or the day on which the individual first satisfies section 1395(b) of this title, whichever is later.section 426–1(b)(2) of this titleJanuary 1, 2023October 1, 2022o

An individual described in paragraph (1) whose entitlement for hospital insurance benefits under part A ends by reason of on or after , shall be deemed to have enrolled in the medical insurance program established by this part for purposes of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs.section 426–1(b)(2) of this titleJanuary 1, 2023

The Secretary shall establish a process under which an individual described in paragraph (1) whose other coverage described in section 1395(b)(2)(A) of this title, or coverage under this part (including the medical insurance program established under this part for purposes of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs), is terminated voluntarily or involuntary may enroll or reenroll, if applicable, in the medical insurance program established under this part for purposes of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs.o1So in original. Probably should be “involuntarily”.1

children.heading: Special enrollment period for certain Postal Service annuitants and family members
children.label: o Special enrollment period for certain Postal Service annuitants and family members
i
children.text: a Postal Service annuitant who is entitled to benefits under part A of this subchapter, but excluding an individual who is eligible to enroll under such part under or 1395i–2a of this title; orsection 1395i–2 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a member of family (as defined in ) of a Postal Service annuitant and is entitled to benefits under part A of this subchapter, but excluding an individual who is eligible to enroll under such part under or 1395i–2a of this title; andsection 8901(5) of title 5section 1395i–2 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled under this part, the individual may elect to be enrolled under this part during a special enrollment period during the 6-month period beginning on .April 1, 2024

In this subsection, the term “Postal Service annuitant” means an annuitant enrolled in a health benefits plan under chapter 89 of title 5, whose Government contribution is required to be paid under section 8906(g)(2) of such title.
